AccountStatus.description: Lifecycle status of an account, per Berlin Group PSD2 `accountStatus`.
AccountStatus.title: AccountStatus

AccountType.description:
Type of bank account.

Aligned with the ISO 20022 ExternalCashAccountType1Code values exposed
via the Berlin Group PSD2 `cashAccountType`, collapsed to the buckets
that map cleanly across regions and reflect distinctions a user would
actually ask about:

  - `Current`  - day-to-day transaction account (ISO 20022 CACC).
                 Debit cards and stored-value / prepaid cards live
                 here too: `maskedPan` flags an attached card and the
                 balance/availableBalance pair answers "can I use it?"
  - `Savings`  - interest-bearing savings (ISO 20022 SVGS)
  - `Credit`   - revolving credit / credit card account; carries the
                 statement-cycle fields below (`statementBalance`,
                 `minimumPaymentDue`, `paymentDueDate`, ...).
  - `Loan`     - amortizing loan or mortgage (ISO 20022 LOAN)
  - `Other`    - anything else (escrow, brokerage, etc.)
AccountType.title: AccountType

AccountUsage.description:
How the account is used, per Berlin Group PSD2 `usage`.

`Private` corresponds to PSD2 `PRIV` (personal); `Business` to `ORGA`
(professional / organisation).
AccountUsage.title: AccountUsage

Account.description:
Bank account with balance and metadata.

Property names follow Berlin Group PSD2 `accountDetails` where they
overlap (`iban`, `bban`, `bic`, `maskedPan`, `product`, `status`,
`usage`), so servers fronting a PSD2 backend can map fields directly.
Servers SHOULD populate at least one of `accountNumber`, `iban`,
`bban`, or `maskedPan`.

accountNumber.description: Display-friendly account number in the locally familiar format. For European accounts this is typically the IBAN; otherwise the domestic account number. Use the typed identifier fields (`iban`, `bban`, `maskedPan`) when you need to parse or route on it.
accountNumber.title: Accountnumber

availableBalance.description: Funds available to spend right now in `currency`, after pending authorisations and including any `overdraftLimit` headroom. Aligns with the PSD2 `interimAvailable` balance type.
availableBalance.title: Availablebalance

balance.description: Current booked balance in `currency`. For credit accounts a negative value indicates an outstanding balance owed.
balance.title: Balance

balanceUpdatedAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp at which `balance` and `availableBalance` were last refreshed by the bank.
balanceUpdatedAt.title: Balanceupdatedat

bban.description: Basic Bank Account Number, the domestic identifier used in countries (or for products) without an IBAN.
bban.title: Bban

bic.description: BIC / SWIFT code of the holding bank (ISO 9362).
bic.title: Bic

currency.description: ISO 4217 currency code of the account. For multi-currency accounts (per PSD2 convention) use `XXX`.
currency.title: Currency

iban.description: IBAN (ISO 13616), when the account has one.
iban.title: Iban

isDefaultAccount.description: Whether this is the user's default account for new transfers. At most one account per response SHOULD have this set to true.
isDefaultAccount.title: Isdefaultaccount

isWithdrawalAccount.description: Whether this account can be used as the source of an outgoing transfer or withdrawal.
isWithdrawalAccount.title: Iswithdrawalaccount

maskedPan.description: Masked Primary Account Number for card accounts. The middle digits MUST be obscured.
maskedPan.title: Maskedpan

minimumPaymentDue.description: Credit accounts only. Minimum payment required by `paymentDueDate` to keep the account current and avoid late fees, in `currency`. Servers MUST omit on non-credit accounts.
minimumPaymentDue.title: Minimumpaymentdue

name.description: Human-readable account name set by the bank or the user.
name.title: Name

openedDate.description: Date the account was opened, ISO 8601 (YYYY-MM-DD).
openedDate.title: Openeddate

overdraftLimit.description: Approved overdraft on a current/savings account or credit limit on a credit/card account, in `currency`. `0` (or omitted) means no extra headroom beyond `balance`.
overdraftLimit.title: Overdraftlimit

ownerName.description: Primary account holder's name as the bank holds it. For joint accounts, servers MAY join multiple names with `, `.
ownerName.title: Ownername

paymentDueDate.description: Credit accounts only. ISO 8601 date by which `minimumPaymentDue` must be paid. Servers MUST omit on non-credit accounts.
paymentDueDate.title: Paymentduedate

product.description: Bank's marketing name for the account product, per PSD2 `product`. Free-form and proprietary to the issuer.
product.title: Product

statementBalance.description: Credit accounts only. Total billed on the most recent closed statement, in `currency`. Pay this in full by `paymentDueDate` to avoid interest charges. Servers MUST omit on non-credit accounts.
statementBalance.title: Statementbalance

statementClosingDate.description: Credit accounts only. ISO 8601 date the current billing cycle closes and the next statement is generated. Optional even on credit accounts; servers MUST omit on non-credit accounts.
statementClosingDate.title: Statementclosingdate

status.description: Lifecycle status. If omitted, clients SHOULD treat the account as `Enabled`.

usage.description: Whether this is a personal or business account.

Account.title: Account

isFavorite.description: Whether this recipient is marked as favorite
isFavorite.title: Isfavorite

name.description: Recipient's full name or business name
name.title: Name

socialSecurityNumber.description: National ID / SSN (format varies by country)
socialSecurityNumber.title: Socialsecuritynumber

AccountNumberIdentifier.description: Country-specific composite for non-IBAN markets (US, UK pre-IBAN, etc.).

accountNumber.description: Account number in the country's native format.
accountNumber.title: Accountnumber

routing.description: US ABA routing number, when applicable.
routing.title: Routing

AccountNumberIdentifier.title: AccountNumberIdentifier

AliasIdentifier.description: Alias-based identifier (UPI VPA, Pix key, email-routed payments, etc.).

alias.description: The alias value as the user knows it.
alias.title: Alias

AliasIdentifier.title: AliasIdentifier

AliasType.description: Type tag for alias-based account identifiers (UPI VPA, Pix key, email-routed payments, etc.).
AliasType.title: AliasType

BbanIdentifier.description: Domestic Basic Bank Account Number, used in markets without an IBAN.

bban.description: Domestic account identifier in the country's native format.
bban.title: Bban

BbanIdentifier.title: BbanIdentifier

type.description: Hint for the kind of identifier. bank2ai does not validate the value.

value.description: National identifier value, in the country's native format.
value.title: Value

NationalIdType.description:
Opaque label for the kind of national identifier carried in `NationalId.value`.

bank2ai does not validate national-ID formats; this is a hint so
clients can render or route appropriately. Servers SHOULD set the
closest-matching value or use `other`.
NationalIdType.title: NationalIdType

Party.description:
Counterparty in a transaction or transfer.

Profile of: ISO 20022 `PartyIdentification135` (subset).

anyOf.description: Discriminated union of typed account identifiers. Profile of: ISO 20022 `AccountIdentification4Choice`.

bic.description: BIC / SWIFT code of the party's bank, ISO 9362.
bic.title: Bic

name.description: Party's full name or business name.
name.title: Name

Party.title: Party

RemittanceInformation.description:
Free-text and/or structured remittance information for a transaction.

Profile of: ISO 20022 `RemittanceInformation`. Servers SHOULD populate
this only when remittance info is genuinely present and not just a
duplicate of `Transaction.description`.

creditorReference.description: Structured creditor reference, typically an ISO 11649 RF-prefixed reference. The reference is opaque to bank2ai; servers pass it through as the bank exposes it.
creditorReference.title: Creditorreference

unstructured.description: Free-text remittance line (e.g., the SEPA `RemittanceInformation/Unstructured` field).
unstructured.title: Unstructured

RemittanceInformation.title: RemittanceInformation

TransactionCode.description:
ISO 20022 BankTransactionCode taxonomy.

Three-level hierarchy (`domain` / `family` / `subFamily`). Servers
MAY emit only `domain` if that is all the bank exposes; the deeper
levels are populated when known.

family.description: Mid-level family (e.g., `RCDT` for received credit transfers).
family.title: Family

TransactionCode.title: TransactionCode

TransactionStatus.description:
Booking status of a transaction, per Berlin Group PSD2 `bookingStatus`.

`Booked` is the default a client should assume when servers omit
`Transaction.status`.
TransactionStatus.title: TransactionStatus

Transaction.description:
Financial transaction with date, amount and metadata.

Profile of: ISO 20022 `EntryDetails2` and Berlin Group PSD2
`transactions` array element.

accountId.description: References the `id` of the `Account` this transaction belongs to.
accountId.title: Accountid

amount.description: Transaction amount in the user's default currency (negative for expenses, positive for income). Clients SHOULD render this without a currency symbol unless the user has explicitly asked which currency a transaction is in.
amount.title: Amount

bookingDate.description: Date the transaction posted to the account, ISO 8601 (YYYY-MM-DD).
bookingDate.title: Bookingdate

categoryId.description: Category id (the `id` field from get-categories which also has category name).
categoryId.title: Categoryid

categoryRaw.description: Bank-native category label as the upstream system exposes it. Useful when `categoryId` is mapped from something more specific.
categoryRaw.title: Categoryraw

counterparty.description: Typed counterparty record. Servers SHOULD populate when more than just `counterpartyName` is known (account identifier, BIC, national id).

counterpartyName.description: Best-effort merchant or counterparty display name. Distinct from `description`, which is the bank's narrative as presented to the user.
counterpartyName.title: Counterpartyname

description.description: Transaction description, merchant name or recipient name
description.title: Description

endToEndId.description: ISO 20022 cross-rail end-to-end identifier preserved across the payment chain.
endToEndId.title: Endtoendid

originalAmount.description: Transaction amount in `originalCurrency` (negative for expenses, positive for income). Present only when `originalCurrency` is set.
originalAmount.title: Originalamount

originalCurrency.description: ISO 4217 currency code of the original transaction, present only when the transaction was made in a currency other than the user's default. Pair with `originalAmount` to recover the original amount.
originalCurrency.title: Originalcurrency

remittanceInformation.description: Free-text and/or structured remittance info attached to the transaction. Servers SHOULD omit when it would duplicate `description`.

status.description: Booking status. If omitted, clients SHOULD treat the transaction as `Booked`.

transactionCode.description: ISO 20022 BankTransactionCode classification of the entry.

valueDate.description: Date the funds become available, ISO 8601. Servers SHOULD omit when equal to `bookingDate`. Profile of: ISO 20022 `ValueDate`.
valueDate.title: Valuedate

Transaction.title: Transaction

tools.description: Create a new payment recipient with their name, account number, and national ID. The recipient can then be used for transfers.

account_number.description: Recipient's bank account number (format varies by country).

kennitala.description: Icelandic national ID for the recipient, if known.

name.description: Recipient's full name or business name.

tools.name: create-recipient

outputSchema.description: Result of creating a payment recipient

isFavorite.description: Whether this recipient is marked as favorite

name.description: Recipient's full name or business name

socialSecurityNumber.description: National ID / SSN (format varies by country)

item.description: The created recipient when creation succeeded.

tools.description: Execute a money transfer after the user has confirmed the details. Use prepare-transfer-icelandic first to prepare and validate.

description.description: Free-text note shown on the recipient's statement.

tools.name: execute-transfer

anyOf.description: Bank-issued receipt for an executed transfer.

status.description: Execution status reported by the bank.

timestamp.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the bank recorded the transfer.

item.description: Receipt details when the transfer was accepted by the bank.

tools.description: Get the user's bank accounts and cards. Returns each account with balances, identifiers (account number, plus IBAN/BBAN/BIC or masked PAN where the bank has them), holder, product, type, lifecycle status, and usage. Field shapes follow the Berlin Group PSD2 `accountDetails` model where they overlap.

account_type.description: Filter by account type. `Current` and `Savings` are the common spending and deposit accounts; `Credit` covers revolving credit / credit cards; `Loan` covers mortgages and amortizing loans. Debit and prepaid cards live under `Current`; their `maskedPan` field flags the attached card.

only_withdrawal_accounts.description: If true, return only accounts usable as the source of an outgoing transfer or withdrawal.

status.description: Filter by lifecycle status. Defaults to all statuses; pass `Enabled` to hide closed or blocked accounts.

usage.description: Filter by usage: personal (`Private`) or business (`Business`). Servers MAY ignore this if they do not track usage.

tools.name: get-accounts

outputSchema.description: Envelope for a list of accounts

items.description:
Bank account with balance and metadata.

Property names follow Berlin Group PSD2 `accountDetails` where they
overlap (`iban`, `bban`, `bic`, `maskedPan`, `product`, `status`,
`usage`), so servers fronting a PSD2 backend can map fields directly.
Servers SHOULD populate at least one of `accountNumber`, `iban`,
`bban`, or `maskedPan`.

accountNumber.description: Display-friendly account number in the locally familiar format. For European accounts this is typically the IBAN; otherwise the domestic account number. Use the typed identifier fields (`iban`, `bban`, `maskedPan`) when you need to parse or route on it.

anyOf.description:
Type of bank account.

Aligned with the ISO 20022 ExternalCashAccountType1Code values exposed
via the Berlin Group PSD2 `cashAccountType`, collapsed to the buckets
that map cleanly across regions and reflect distinctions a user would
actually ask about:

  - `Current`  - day-to-day transaction account (ISO 20022 CACC).
                 Debit cards and stored-value / prepaid cards live
                 here too: `maskedPan` flags an attached card and the
                 balance/availableBalance pair answers "can I use it?"
  - `Savings`  - interest-bearing savings (ISO 20022 SVGS)
  - `Credit`   - revolving credit / credit card account; carries the
                 statement-cycle fields below (`statementBalance`,
                 `minimumPaymentDue`, `paymentDueDate`, ...).
  - `Loan`     - amortizing loan or mortgage (ISO 20022 LOAN)
  - `Other`    - anything else (escrow, brokerage, etc.)

availableBalance.description: Funds available to spend right now in `currency`, after pending authorisations and including any `overdraftLimit` headroom. Aligns with the PSD2 `interimAvailable` balance type.

balance.description: Current booked balance in `currency`. For credit accounts a negative value indicates an outstanding balance owed.

balanceUpdatedAt.description: ISO 8601 timestamp at which `balance` and `availableBalance` were last refreshed by the bank.

bban.description: Basic Bank Account Number, the domestic identifier used in countries (or for products) without an IBAN.

bic.description: BIC / SWIFT code of the holding bank (ISO 9362).

currency.description: ISO 4217 currency code of the account. For multi-currency accounts (per PSD2 convention) use `XXX`.

iban.description: IBAN (ISO 13616), when the account has one.

isDefaultAccount.description: Whether this is the user's default account for new transfers. At most one account per response SHOULD have this set to true.

isWithdrawalAccount.description: Whether this account can be used as the source of an outgoing transfer or withdrawal.

maskedPan.description: Masked Primary Account Number for card accounts. The middle digits MUST be obscured.

minimumPaymentDue.description: Credit accounts only. Minimum payment required by `paymentDueDate` to keep the account current and avoid late fees, in `currency`. Servers MUST omit on non-credit accounts.

name.description: Human-readable account name set by the bank or the user.

openedDate.description: Date the account was opened, ISO 8601 (YYYY-MM-DD).

overdraftLimit.description: Approved overdraft on a current/savings account or credit limit on a credit/card account, in `currency`. `0` (or omitted) means no extra headroom beyond `balance`.

ownerName.description: Primary account holder's name as the bank holds it. For joint accounts, servers MAY join multiple names with `, `.

paymentDueDate.description: Credit accounts only. ISO 8601 date by which `minimumPaymentDue` must be paid. Servers MUST omit on non-credit accounts.

product.description: Bank's marketing name for the account product, per PSD2 `product`. Free-form and proprietary to the issuer.

statementBalance.description: Credit accounts only. Total billed on the most recent closed statement, in `currency`. Pay this in full by `paymentDueDate` to avoid interest charges. Servers MUST omit on non-credit accounts.

statementClosingDate.description: Credit accounts only. ISO 8601 date the current billing cycle closes and the next statement is generated. Optional even on credit accounts; servers MUST omit on non-credit accounts.

anyOf.description: Lifecycle status of an account, per Berlin Group PSD2 `accountStatus`.

status.description: Lifecycle status. If omitted, clients SHOULD treat the account as `Enabled`.

anyOf.description:
How the account is used, per Berlin Group PSD2 `usage`.

`Private` corresponds to PSD2 `PRIV` (personal); `Business` to `ORGA`
(professional / organisation).

usage.description: Whether this is a personal or business account.

tools.description: Get transaction categories. Returns a list of categories that transactions can be classified into.
tools.name: get-categories

outputSchema.description: Envelope for a list of categories

tools.description: Get saved payment recipients filtered by name. Returns matching recipients with their account details.

name.description: Free-text search; matches partial names of saved recipients.

tools.name: get-recipients

outputSchema.description: Envelope for a list of recipients

isFavorite.description: Whether this recipient is marked as favorite

name.description: Recipient's full name or business name

socialSecurityNumber.description: National ID / SSN (format varies by country)

tools.description: Get bank transactions. Returns a list of transactions with amounts, dates, descriptions, and categories.

account_ids.description: Restrict to transactions on these account.id values (from get-accounts).

category_ids.description: Restrict to these category ids (the `id` field from get-categories).

count.description: Maximum number of transactions to return.

cursor.description: Opaque pagination cursor returned as `nextCursor` from a previous call. Omit to fetch the first page.

max_amount.description: Inclusive upper bound on the transaction amount. Amounts are signed: expenses are negative, income is positive. Examples: `max_amount=0` keeps only expenses; `max_amount=-50` keeps only expenses of 50 or more (in absolute value).

min_amount.description: Inclusive lower bound on the transaction amount. Amounts are signed: expenses are negative, income is positive. Examples: `min_amount=0` keeps only income; `min_amount=-100` drops expenses larger than 100.

tools.name: get-transactions

outputSchema.description: Envelope for a list of transactions

items.description:
Financial transaction with date, amount and metadata.

Profile of: ISO 20022 `EntryDetails2` and Berlin Group PSD2
`transactions` array element.

accountId.description: References the `id` of the `Account` this transaction belongs to.

amount.description: Transaction amount in the user's default currency (negative for expenses, positive for income). Clients SHOULD render this without a currency symbol unless the user has explicitly asked which currency a transaction is in.

bookingDate.description: Date the transaction posted to the account, ISO 8601 (YYYY-MM-DD).

categoryId.description: Category id (the `id` field from get-categories which also has category name).

categoryRaw.description: Bank-native category label as the upstream system exposes it. Useful when `categoryId` is mapped from something more specific.

anyOf.description:
Counterparty in a transaction or transfer.

Profile of: ISO 20022 `PartyIdentification135` (subset).
Discriminated union of typed account identifiers. Profile of: ISO 20022 `AccountIdentification4Choice`.

oneOf.description: Domestic Basic Bank Account Number, used in markets without an IBAN.

bban.description: Domestic account identifier in the country's native format.

oneOf.description: Country-specific composite for non-IBAN markets (US, UK pre-IBAN, etc.).

accountNumber.description: Account number in the country's native format.

routing.description: US ABA routing number, when applicable.

oneOf.description: Alias-based identifier (UPI VPA, Pix key, email-routed payments, etc.).

alias.description: The alias value as the user knows it.

bic.description: BIC / SWIFT code of the party's bank, ISO 9362.

name.description: Party's full name or business name.

anyOf.description:
Opaque label for the kind of national identifier carried in `NationalId.value`.

bank2ai does not validate national-ID formats; this is a hint so
clients can render or route appropriately. Servers SHOULD set the
closest-matching value or use `other`.

type.description: Hint for the kind of identifier. bank2ai does not validate the value.

value.description: National identifier value, in the country's native format.

counterparty.description: Typed counterparty record. Servers SHOULD populate when more than just `counterpartyName` is known (account identifier, BIC, national id).

counterpartyName.description: Best-effort merchant or counterparty display name. Distinct from `description`, which is the bank's narrative as presented to the user.

description.description: Transaction description, merchant name or recipient name

endToEndId.description: ISO 20022 cross-rail end-to-end identifier preserved across the payment chain.

originalAmount.description: Transaction amount in `originalCurrency` (negative for expenses, positive for income). Present only when `originalCurrency` is set.

originalCurrency.description: ISO 4217 currency code of the original transaction, present only when the transaction was made in a currency other than the user's default. Pair with `originalAmount` to recover the original amount.

anyOf.description:
Free-text and/or structured remittance information for a transaction.

Profile of: ISO 20022 `RemittanceInformation`. Servers SHOULD populate
this only when remittance info is genuinely present and not just a
duplicate of `Transaction.description`.

creditorReference.description: Structured creditor reference, typically an ISO 11649 RF-prefixed reference. The reference is opaque to bank2ai; servers pass it through as the bank exposes it.

unstructured.description: Free-text remittance line (e.g., the SEPA `RemittanceInformation/Unstructured` field).

remittanceInformation.description: Free-text and/or structured remittance info attached to the transaction. Servers SHOULD omit when it would duplicate `description`.

anyOf.description:
Booking status of a transaction, per Berlin Group PSD2 `bookingStatus`.

`Booked` is the default a client should assume when servers omit
`Transaction.status`.

status.description: Booking status. If omitted, clients SHOULD treat the transaction as `Booked`.

anyOf.description:
ISO 20022 BankTransactionCode taxonomy.

Three-level hierarchy (`domain` / `family` / `subFamily`). Servers
MAY emit only `domain` if that is all the bank exposes; the deeper
levels are populated when known.

family.description: Mid-level family (e.g., `RCDT` for received credit transfers).

transactionCode.description: ISO 20022 BankTransactionCode classification of the entry.

valueDate.description: Date the funds become available, ISO 8601. Servers SHOULD omit when equal to `bookingDate`. Profile of: ISO 20022 `ValueDate`.

nextCursor.description: Opaque cursor to pass back as `cursor` to fetch the next page. Absent or null when there are no more results.

tools.description: Get an aggregated summary of transactions, scoped to either income or expenses. Returns totals, counts, and averages, optionally grouped by category, month, or both. Filters mirror get-transactions: account, date, amount range, category ids.

account_ids.description: Restrict to transactions on these account.id values (from get-accounts).

category_ids.description: Restrict to these category ids (the `id` field from get-categories).

direction.description: Restrict to income (positive amounts) or expenses (negative amounts). A summary covers exactly one direction; call the tool twice to compare.

group_by.description: Aggregation key. `none` returns a single row spanning all matched transactions; `category` groups by category id; `month` groups by calendar month (YYYY-MM); `both` groups by (category id, month) pairs. Each summary row reports `categoryId` and/or `month` accordingly.

max_amount.description: Inclusive upper bound on the transaction amount. Amounts are signed: expenses are negative, income is positive. Combined with `direction`, both filters are applied.

min_amount.description: Inclusive lower bound on the transaction amount. Amounts are signed: expenses are negative, income is positive. Combined with `direction`, both filters are applied.

tools.name: get-transactions-summary

period.description: Date range covered by the aggregation.

summary.description: One entry per aggregation key value.

items.description: One row of an aggregated transactions summary.

averageAmount.description: Mean transaction amount within this group.

categoryId.description: Category id when the request grouped by `category` or `both`; null otherwise. Resolve to a name via get-categories.

month.description: ISO 8601 month (YYYY-MM) when the request grouped by `month` or `both`; null otherwise.

totalAmount.description: Sum of transaction amounts in this group.

transactionCount.description: Number of transactions contributing to this group.

total.description: Sum across all groups in the summary.

tools.description: Prepare a domestic Icelandic money transfer. Validates recipient and prepares transfer details for confirmation.

amount.description: Transfer amount in the source account's currency.

currency.description: ISO 4217 currency code; if empty, source-account currency is used.

description.description: Free-text note shown on the recipient's statement.

withdrawal_account_number.description: Source account number; if empty, the user's default withdrawal account is used.

tools.name: prepare-transfer-icelandic

actions.description: Optional follow-up actions the client may surface.

items.description: Suggested follow-up action for a prepared transfer.

link.description: Target URL or in-app link to perform the action.

anyOf.description: Validated transfer details awaiting user confirmation.

description.description: Free-text note shown on the recipient's statement.

recipient_name.description: Recipient's full name or business name.

withdrawal_account.description: Resolved source account snapshot used for the transfer.

accountNumber.description: Display-friendly account number in the locally familiar format. For European accounts this is typically the IBAN; otherwise the domestic account number. Use the typed identifier fields (`iban`, `bban`, `maskedPan`) when you need to parse or route on it.

Type of bank account.

Aligned with the ISO 20022 ExternalCashAccountType1Code values exposed
via the Berlin Group PSD2 `cashAccountType`, collapsed to the buckets
that map cleanly across regions and reflect distinctions a user would
actually ask about:

  - `Current`  - day-to-day transaction account (ISO 20022 CACC).
                 Debit cards and stored-value / prepaid cards live
                 here too: `maskedPan` flags an attached card and the

  - `Savings`  - interest-bearing savings (ISO 20022 SVGS)
  - `Credit`   - revolving credit / credit card account; carries the
                 statement-cycle fields below (`statementBalance`,
                 `minimumPaymentDue`, `paymentDueDate`, ...).
  - `Loan`     - amortizing loan or mortgage (ISO 20022 LOAN)
  - `Other`    - anything else (escrow, brokerage, etc.)

balance.description: Current booked balance in `currency`. For credit accounts a negative value indicates an outstanding balance owed.
